pick.rationale: Wisconsin holds a commanding advantage as a top-25 team ranked 21st in Torvik and 22nd in KenPom compared to High Point's 93rd/92nd rankings. The Badgers' elite offensive production combined with a +23.4 efficiency margin nearly triple that of High Point creates a clear mismatch. In a classic 5/12 seed matchup where five seeds historically win approximately 70-75% of the time, Wisconsin's Big Ten pedigree and superior talent depth make them the heavy favorite to advance past the Panthers.

reasoningStep.title: Wisconsin vs High Point Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Advanced metrics and betting lines confirm Wisconsin as the clear favorite in this 5/12 Round of 64 matchup.
reasoningStep.evidence: Wisconsin ranks 21st/22nd in national ratings with elite offense (top 11 nationally) and +23.4 efficiency margin versus High Point's 92nd/93rd rankings and +8.4 margin. BetMGM odds show Wisconsin as 9.5-point favorite (-500 moneyline). Historical 5/12 seed trends favor the higher seed 70-75% of the time.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

Great! Now I have the ratings for both teams:

Wisconsin (5-seed):
- Torvik rank: 21st
- KenPom rank: 22nd
- Record: 24-10
- AdjO: 127.2 (8th nationally) / 125.4 (11th nationally)
- AdjD: 101.6 (48th) / 102 (not ranked in top tier)
- Efficiency margin: +23.4

High Point (12-seed):
- Torvik rank: 93rd
- KenPom rank: 92nd
- Record: 30-4
- AdjO: 115.6 (73rd) / 117.1 (66th)
- AdjD: 107.5 (148th) / 108.7
- Efficiency margin: +8.4

The gap is significant - Wisconsin ranks in the top 25 nationally while High Point is around 90s. Wisconsin's efficiency margin (+23.4) is nearly triple that of High Point (+8.4). Wisconsin has elite offensive production (top 11-13 nationally) while High Point is middle-of-the-pack.

Historically, 5-seeds win about 70-75% of the time against 12-seeds. Wisconsin's superior metrics across the board suggest they should handle this matchup comfortably.
